inline_comments.message:
This is great.

In addition, I would suggest converting this and builder to Kotlin, if you will.
https://www.baeldung.com/kotlin-builder-pattern#kotlin-style

If you think it fits, 1) yeh!  2) it can wait for a different commit.

Must have the @JsonAlias("operational-environment"); not sure how it works with the Json-Creator approach.

The **alias** lets the object serialize to camelCase operationalEnvironment, while deserialize from either dash-separated operational-environment and camelCase.

This is because AAI responds with dash-separated names, but object is forwarded to front-end with camelCase field names.

I'd rather have this done as part of a different change. I think that rewriting only the builder in kotlin would introduce a technological inconsistency and other parts (at least the POJOS) need to be considered.

Thanks for the explanation. I have resolved this by keeping the @JsonProperty and setting its access = Access.WRITE. So now the dash-separated names will only be used during deserialization (json2pojo) while still supporting camelcase. As for serialization everything will work as previously - output json will have camelcase names.
I have added additional unit tests that verify all the above ser/deser scenarios.
